steps.title: Isolate the term with q and divide by 24
steps.content:
Start with $\frac{24}{p}=\frac{24}{q}-\frac{24}{r}-\frac{24}{s}$. Add $\frac{24}{r}+\frac{24}{s}$ to both sides:
$$\frac{24}{q} = \frac{24}{p} + \frac{24}{r} + \frac{24}{s}$$
Divide both sides by 24:
$$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{r} + \frac{1}{s}$$

steps.title: Combine the right side over the common denominator prs
steps.content:
Rewrite each fraction with denominator $prs$:
$$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{rs}{prs} + \frac{ps}{prs} + \frac{pr}{prs} = \frac{pr + ps + rs}{prs}$$
Take the reciprocal of both sides:
$$q = \frac{prs}{pr + ps + rs}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A. $p + r + s$ - this would only work if $\frac{1}{q} = \frac{1}{p+r+s}$, but $\frac{1}{p}+\frac{1}{r}+\frac{1}{s} \neq \frac{1}{p+r+s}$. Eliminated.
  • B. $24(p+r+s)$ - the 24s cancel when we divide both sides by 24, so a factor of 24 should not appear. Eliminated.
  • C. $\frac{prs}{pr+ps+rs}$ - exactly the expression we derived. Correct.
  • D. $\frac{prs}{24p+24r+24s}$ - again introduces a stray factor of 24 in the denominator. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — $\frac{prs}{pr+ps+rs}$- C.
